text: The da Vinci robotic system is docked, and the surgeon begins deflecting the left colon medially towards the upper pole.
The surgeon dissects the upper pole of the adrenal gland free from the undersurface of the spleen, using robotic instruments to carefully separate the tissue.
The surgeon divides the lienorenal ligament, separating it from the surrounding structures.
The surgeon identifies the left renal vein and dresses the adrenal vein, then clips and divides the adrenal vein.
The surgeon uses a monopolar cautery to separate the adrenal gland from the upper pole of the kidney, controlling bleeding and achieving hemostasis in the surgical field.
The surgeon separates the adrenal gland from the posterior abdominal wall, clipping and dividing the vessels between them using Hem-o-lok clips.
The surgeon dissects the adrenal gland away from the kidney and surrounding structures, and the specimen is retrieved upon closure.
